title: check_extension_use returns wrong error kind
description: check_extension_use() (lib.rs ~186) returns SieveErrorKind::UnsupportedExtension(w) when an extension command like fileinto is used without a prior require declaration. UnsupportedExtension means 'this evaluator does not implement extension X', but 'used without require' means 'the script is structurally invalid' — a distinct condition. A caller matching on UnsupportedExtension to detect unsupported extensions will also catch scripts that simply forgot their require line, leading to wrong error messages ('extension not supported' vs 'script missing require'). A contributor adding future extension command checks will copy this pattern. A new SieveErrorKind::MissingRequire(String) variant (or using SieveErrorKind::Parse) would be semantically correct.
